subject: DIRECTIVE: budget model refactored — counter with cutoff replaces depleting pool

body.summary: The autonomy budget model has changed from a depleting pool (budget_current decrements from budget_max) to a spend counter with optional cutoff (budget_spent increments, budget_cutoff sets limit, 0 means unlimited). All code changes committed to operations-agent. Rollout to deployment host and peer agents required.

gate_logic.new: refuse when budget_cutoff > 0 AND budget_spent + cost > budget_cutoff. Cutoff 0 means unlimited — counter-only mode.

api_wire_format.backward_compat: Dashboard client-side shim normalizes old-schema agents: budget_current → budget_spent, budget_max → budget_cutoff

files_changed: internal/config/config.go — no budget config changes needed
internal/server/server.go — status query uses new columns
interagent/worker.js — backward-compat shim + all endpoints updated

steps.action: Build new meshd binary from operations-agent main branch
steps.command: cd ~/projects/operations-agent && git pull && go build -o meshd-ops ./cmd/meshd/

steps.action: Migrate state.db on all 5 agents — rename columns
steps.command_per_agent: sqlite3 state.db "ALTER TABLE autonomy_budget RENAME COLUMN budget_current TO budget_spent; ALTER TABLE autonomy_budget RENAME COLUMN budget_max TO budget_cutoff; UPDATE autonomy_budget SET budget_spent = 0, budget_cutoff = 0;"

steps.action: Swap meshd binary on all 5 agents and restart services
steps.note: The new binary reads budget_spent/budget_cutoff. Old binary would fail on missing columns. Swap + migrate atomically.

steps.action: Verify via /api/status — should show budget_spent and budget_cutoff fields
Deploy updated compositor Worker (interagent/worker.js) to Cloudflare
steps.note: Worker has backward-compat shim — safe to deploy before or after agent migration

body.current_state: All 5 agents currently serve old schema (budget_current/budget_max with 9999 tracking values). New code committed to operations-agent main. No deployment yet.

epistemic_flags: SQLite ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN requires SQLite 3.25.0+ (2018) — verify remote SQLite version
If ALTER fails, alternative: rebuild state.db via bootstrap-state-db.sh (loses existing budget rows but they reset anyway)
